confirmations.reason: CLAUDE.md is precisely a repo/project-scoped markdown file that gives Claude standing instructions and context, loaded automatically — a direct match.
Skills are named, reusable prompt bundles packaging multi-step workflows with supporting files — directly matching 'bundle reusable capabilities/instructions the agent can load.'
Hooks are explicitly user-defined shell commands that fire at specific lifecycle points in Claude Code (e.g., before/after tool calls) — a direct match to lifecycle event hooks.
Output Styles are saved markdown configurations that modify Claude's system prompt, directly realizing the capability to shape system prompt, persona, or output style.
Per-subagent model selection (Haiku, Sonnet, Opus) directly enables choosing which underlying model a subagent runs on — a direct realization of model selection.
MCP is the standard protocol Claude Code uses to connect to external tools, data, and services — a textbook match.
Managed MCP Configuration directly covers deploying, configuring, and scoping a fixed set of MCP servers with allowlists/denylists — directly realizing install, configure, scope, and discover.
MCP Auth Methods explicitly covers env-var, HTTP header, and OAuth 2/2.1 authentication to MCP servers — a direct match to MCP authentication.
MCP Tool Allow-listing provides granular permission control over which MCP tools an agent may invoke, including wildcard rules — directly realizing MCP tool governance/allow-listing.
Auto Memory (MEMORY.md) is notes Claude writes itself from corrections/preferences, persisted per git repo and auto-loaded — directly realizing durable memory kept across turns/sessions.
Automatic context-window compaction during long sessions directly realizes context compaction to extend effective working memory — both manual and auto aspects are implied by 'automatic' compaction.
Subagents are explicitly specialized AI assistants running in isolated context windows handling delegated tasks — a direct match to 'spawn focused sub-agents to handle parts of a task.'
Agent Teams coordinate multiple independent Claude Code sessions led by a team lead with shared work — directly realizing multi-agent teams and messaging/coordination.

Routines are scheduled automations running on cloud infrastructure — they realize scheduled/triggered runs, not the core capability of defining repeatable named multi-step task sequences (workflows/saved automations). The scheduling aspect dominates over the workflow-definition aspect.
Plan Mode explicitly researches and proposes changes without executing or modifying anything — a direct match to 'produce a reviewable plan before the agent edits anything.'

Auto Mode executes actions autonomously with a classifier reviewing each action — this is supervised autonomous execution, not goal-directed iteration where the agent loops until a verifiable 'done' condition is met. The loop-until-condition aspect is absent.
Worktree Isolation runs parallel agents in separate git worktrees/checkouts so they don't overwrite each other — directly realizing parallel isolated workspaces.
Claude Code on the Web runs tasks on Anthropic-managed cloud infrastructure accessible from a browser or phone — a direct match to a managed cloud execution environment.
Background Agent Execution explicitly runs a subagent as a non-blocking background task alongside other operations — directly realizing background/async execution.
Schedule Trigger runs a routine on a recurring cadence or once at a specific future time — a direct match to scheduled/triggered runs.
Sandbox Runtime wraps the Claude Code process in Seatbelt/bubblewrap isolation — directly realizing sandboxed execution to limit blast radius.
The Permission System classifies tools by approval requirements and governs whether Claude Code may use them — directly realizing tool permission controls (approve/deny what the agent can do).
Egress Firewall Control uses a firewall script to restrict outbound network traffic to explicitly allowed domains — a direct match to restricting the agent's outbound network access.
Prompt Injection Protection includes safeguards against malicious text overriding Claude's instructions — a direct match.
Security Guidance Plugin makes Claude review its own code changes for vulnerabilities across deterministic and AI-based checks — directly realizing security scanning of agent actions/code.
Dev Container Integration runs Claude Code inside a Docker container managed by VS Code with the project mounted, so commands execute in an isolated container runtime — a direct match.
OpenTelemetry Monitoring exports metrics, events, and traces via OpenTelemetry to OTLP-compatible backends — directly realizing audit logging/telemetry for review and compliance.
Automated PR Code Review uses multi-agent analysis to flag logic errors and security vulnerabilities in PRs — directly realizing automated code review of diffs/PRs.
Cost Tracking tracks spend and per-session token accounting — directly realizing cost and usage tracking.
File checkpointing tracks file modifications made by agent tools during a session, allowing rollback — directly realizing checkpoint/rewind (save points to undo agent changes).

Mid-Session Workflow Resume resumes a paused workflow in the same session, reusing cached results — this is intra-session resumption/caching, not resuming or forking a previous completed/separate session. It's closer to context caching than session resume/fork.
Remote Control lets you continue a local Claude Code session from a phone, tablet, or browser, with monitoring and steering — directly realizing remote control from mobile/web.
Mobile Push Notifications sends push alerts to mobile devices for remote session events and updates — directly realizing push notifications on run progress/completion.
Claude Code in Slack lets users delegate coding tasks from a Slack workspace by mentioning @Claude — directly realizing driving the agent from a chat surface (Slack).

Multi-Surface Connector Support describes where connectors (MCP-like integrations) are available across surfaces — this is about connector availability, not the agent's own access surfaces (terminal, IDE, web, cloud, mobile). It's a tool-connectivity feature, not an agent access-surface feature.
